Disconnected.
Ready to connect to your car?

Insert your OBD2 adapter into the car's diagnostic port
Pair adapter with your phone in the phone settings
Open Voltage again
Grant app permissions for bluetooth
Select your adapter
Press 'Connect'

Troubleshooting:
Make sure you have selected the correct bluetooth adapter
Don't use Voltage together with other OBD2 apps like Car Scanner, Torque, etc simultaneously
Make sure your phone and adapter are in the bluetooth operation range
If you still experience issues: unplug and plug adapter back, if still doesn't work: forget adapter in Android Bluetooth settings, pair it again

Disclaimer:
This app is designed to read data from your vehicle.While the developers taken measures to ensure its safe operation, they are not responsible for any damage to your vehicle or any related issues that may arise during its use. Use this app at your own risk.
We collect non-personal data to improve your experience with our app. This includes: model, battery capacity, mileage, internal resistance and other data.
By using this app, you agree to the collection and use of this data as described in our Privacy Policy. You can opt out in settings

What's new:
- Added ability to set 8 / 12 Amps in Charge Control
- Improved connection stability and ability to reconnect
- App now doesn't make a deep scan for DTCs by default. You can enable it in settings.
- Minor fixes

If you experience issues please press (...) in the upper right corner and select Report Issue / Send Logs (it would be nice if you add some description text)

This chart shows dependency between battery internal resistance and battery temperature. The blue line shows average dependency for this model and year, the orange shows your car's dependency.
The lower the orange chart the better the battery state.
The orange line will appear as you scan your car more often

Get valuable insights into the BECM (Battery Energy Control Module) — a critical component in General Motors EVs. Understand its condition and find out whether your module may be at risk of failure.

Gain insights into your High Voltage charging system isolation — a critical parameter for safe and efficient charging. Ensuring it stays within the proper range is essential for your vehicle to charge and operate normally.
Get access to charge control function. Start immediately or choose departure time. You can also set up a SoC % at which application will attempt to stop the charging process
Get access to engine control function. Emulate hold mode on 2011 Volt, Stop engine from running in a cold weather (ERDTT), Combine SPORT mode and Engine ON...

This screen captures subsequent measurements of the cell delta (the difference between the lowest and highest cell voltage) when the new value is greater than the previous one.

It is intended for use with a depleted battery and while the battery is under significant load (driving at highway speeds for Gen2, or turning the heater to HI for Gen1 / Ampera).

Select temporary departure time for TOMORROW

Monitor potential weak cells in your vehicle’s battery. A smart algorithm detects any ongoing increase in the cell delta — a key parameter that indicates the difference between the highest and lowest cell voltages. For deeper insights, the system also logs cell number, cell voltage, and real-time load (power).

Turning off the engine may cause your car to 'lock' the battery to protect it from damage. Use this feature with caution. App developers are not responsible for any consequences from using this function
